meta.note: Single source of truth for the INLAND opportunity pipeline. Written by the Scout (Claude), edited by collaborators via the dashboard, and read by the Producer (Claude). Do not rename fields without updating the dashboard and the _inland playbooks.

opportunities.title: Let's try to find something doable in Romania
opportunities.eligibility: EU-based cultural entity; project must promote Romanian culture internationally (published/distributed outside Romania). INLAND’s Romania edition — a print magazine for international audiences — qualifies directly.
opportunities.fitRationale: CANTEMIR funds visual arts including photography, targets international promotion of Romanian culture — INLAND’s Romania edition is a direct fit; budget covers production and publication.
opportunities.territory: Romania — Maramureș folk culture & wooden churches, Danube Delta Lipovan fishing communities, Transylvanian Saxon villages, transhumance shepherds (UNESCO ICH 2023)
requiredDocs: Partner letter from Romanian cultural institution
Proof of prior international cultural activity (published issues)

opportunities.title: Goethe-Institut — Africa–Europe Partnerships for Culture (Visual Arts / Mobility)
opportunities.budget: Mobility up to €4,000 (travel); Visual Arts strand larger (production)
opportunities.eligibility: Artists/cultural professionals collaborating between Sub-Saharan Africa and Europe.
opportunities.fitRationale: Direct territory match for the in-progress Rwanda edition; funds Africa–Europe co-creation. Mobility = scouting trip; Visual Arts = production.

changes.note: Producer started: Angola edition, Africa–Europe Visual Arts/Mobility strand

Drafted full INLAND Angola asset set + Goethe application docs (working title 'The Fire That Stays Lit'): deep-research report, 12 selected leads, Topics+Contacts xlsx, pitch (pptx/html), overview PDF, project description/partners/budget/timeline. Desk research; named custodians, partner confirmation and budget to confirm in the field. For review before submission.

opportunities.title: Goethe-Institut — Africa–Europe Partnerships for Culture (Visual Arts / Mobility)
opportunities.budget: Mobility up to €4,000 (travel); Visual Arts strand larger (production)
opportunities.eligibility: Artists/cultural professionals collaborating between Sub-Saharan Africa and Europe.
opportunities.fitRationale: Direct territory match for the in-progress Rwanda edition; funds Africa–Europe co-creation. Mobility = scouting trip; Visual Arts = production.
opportunities.notes: Quarterly mobility deadlines (next 15 Sep 2026); Visual Arts strand within €8M 2025–28 programme.

changes.note: Producer started: Rwanda edition, Africa–Europe Visual Arts strand
Drafted full INLAND Rwanda asset set + application docs for the Africa–Europe Visual Arts/Mobility strands. For review before submission.

opportunities.title: Goethe-Institut — International Coproduction Fund (IKF), Round 2
opportunities.eligibility: Cross-border collaboration with a German/Goethe partner; INLAND qualifies as EU producer with a co-production partner.
opportunities.fitRationale: Producer review: the IKF funds PERFORMING ARTS ONLY (music, theatre, dance, performance) and explicitly excludes film/visual-arts/publishing — so a standard INLAND magazine edition does not qualify. Only viable as a reframed performing-arts co-production (e.g. Bhand Pather / Dard-Shina music) with a Germany-based theatre partner. Recommend redirecting to the Goethe Africa–Europe Visual Arts fund. See research brief, Producer review.
opportunities.territory: Open — pairs with a new edition co-produced with a local partner

changes.note: Producer started: IKF R2 × Kashmir edition

Stopped: IKF is performing-arts-only per official Goethe guidelines; magazine edition ineligible. Reverted; recommend Africa–Europe fund or a reframed performing-arts co-production. No assets produced.

opportunities.title: Institut français — Mediterranean photography support (publication + production)
opportunities.budget: Publication: editing & printing costs covered in full; Production: support for new photographic work (Mediterranean theme)
opportunities.eligibility: Open to all professional photographers who have already exhibited or published, regardless of age or nationality; project must take the Mediterranean world as its theme. INLAND (Italy-based) qualifies.
opportunities.fitRationale: Nationality-open, theme-locked to the Mediterranean, and it covers INLAND's exact cost centres — editing/printing a finished edition (publication) or a fresh field shoot (production). Issue 03 (Isole Minori) already proves the register.
opportunities.territory: Mediterranean world (open) — candidate angles: northern Morocco/Rif–Tétouan; Tunisian islands; southern Italy/Basilicata
requiredDocs: Photographic project / image edit (completed for publication strand)
Editorial / publication project description + Mediterranean-theme statement

changes.note: Producer started: Tunisia edition for the Institut français Mediterranean photography support (publication + production strands)

Drafted full INLAND Tunisia asset set + Institut français application docs (working title 'The Palm in the Sea'): deep-research report, 12 selected leads, Topics+Contacts xlsx, pitch (pptx/html), overview PDF, and application (project description + Mediterranean-theme statement, photographic project/image edit, track record, budget, timeline). Desk research; named custodians, IF strand/routing and budget to confirm. For review before submission.

opportunities.title: Anna Lindh Foundation — Mediterranean Capitals of Culture & Dialogue 2026 (2nd Call, Matera & Tétouan)
opportunities.eligibility: Intercultural entities from the 43 Euro-Med countries running cultural/intercultural activities in Matera (Italy) or Tétouan (Morocco), non-profit, no ticket sales. INLAND (Italy) eligible.
opportunities.fitRationale: Both cities are genuine INLAND territory (Matera/Basilicata; Tétouan→Rif), but the call funds participatory city activities/dialogue, not a publication, and the budget is small — best as a pilot/relationship + scouting foothold, not edition funding. URGENT: deadline 4 Jul 2026.
requiredDocs: Work plan & budget (≤ €5,000, non-profit, no ticketing)

opportunities.title: W. Eugene Smith Grant in Humanistic Photography 2026
opportunities.budget: US$30,000 main grant + two US$10,000 finalist grants
opportunities.eligibility: Open to photographers of any nationality; honours long-form humanistic documentary in the tradition of W. Eugene Smith. INLAND applies via/with a commissioned photographer on an edition's body of work.
opportunities.fitRationale: Humanistic, long-term documentary essay about the human condition — INLAND's exact register; production-scale budget. Applicant is the photographer, so INLAND partners with / nominates its edition photographer. Deadline recurs ~late Sept (call opens ~1 July); confirm when the 2026 cycle posts.
opportunities.territory: Open — pairs with any in-progress INLAND edition (the photographer's body of work on its custodians)
requiredDocs: Portfolio of past work (prior cohesive project)

changes.note: Producer started: W. Eugene Smith Grant application anchored on a Georgia edition (Issue 02 as the photographer's prior cohesive body of work; a proposed humanistic continuation among Georgia's highland custodians).

Drafted full INLAND Georgia asset set + W. Eugene Smith application (working title 'The Quiet Country'): deep-research report (16 leads), 12 selected leads, Topics+Contacts xlsx, pitch (pptx/html), overview PDF, and Smith application docs (project statement within the 2000/3000-char limits, portfolio-of-past-work spec on Issue 02, 40-frame proposed image edit, bio/CV template, checklist/fee, budget, timeline). Desk research; named custodians, the applicant photographer, consents, 2026 deadline/fee and field windows to confirm. For review before submission.

opportunities.title: Lucie Foundation Scholarship 2026 (Documentary / Photojournalism)
opportunities.budget: Cash US$3,000 (Professional) / US$1,000 (Emerging) + camera gear; group exhibition touring House of Lucie galleries incl. Ostuni (Italy)
opportunities.eligibility: Open worldwide to professional and emerging photographers 18+; dedicated Documentary/Photojournalism category. INLAND-commissioned photographer eligible.
opportunities.fitRationale: Documentary/photojournalism category fits INLAND's register; budget is modest (a prize, not edition funding), but the touring exhibition — including Ostuni (Italy) — is a useful visibility/relationship foothold and the entry is low-burden. Deadline to confirm (winners announced Oct 2026; call is live now).
opportunities.territory: Open — any INLAND edition's documentary body of work

opportunities.title: Carmignac Photojournalism Award — 15th Edition (Southeast Asia)
opportunities.budget: €50,000 production grant + travelling exhibition and monograph (premiered at Visa pour l'Image)
opportunities.eligibility: Open to photographers of all nationalities, individual or collaborative; INLAND-eligible via a commissioned photographer. 2026 edition theme/territory is fixed: Southeast Asia.
opportunities.fitRationale: Big budget (€50k) and a real territory (SE Asia coastal/fishing communities hold rich living traditions), BUT the 15th-edition brief is investigative reporting on illegal fishing and human/environmental rights violations — an adversarial/exposé register that clashes with INLAND's 'continuity over crisis, tension not attack' discipline. Stretch; surfaced for the humans to weigh. Recommend not pursuing unless an INLAND-register angle on the set theme is found.
opportunities.territory: Southeast Asia (coastal/fishing communities) — set by the 15th-edition theme
requiredDocs: Project proposal on the set theme

opportunities.title: Allianz Kulturstiftung — "The Art of Bringing Europe Together" (translocal project grant)
opportunities.budget: €40,000–€80,000 (max 50% of total project costs; co-financing required)
opportunities.eligibility: European cultural actors running translocal projects (partners from different countries/regions); funds non-profit partners and projects. INLAND qualifies via a non-profit partner + a cross-border partner.
opportunities.fitRationale: Real budget and a rolling door; the 'translocal / across borders' frame is a natural home for an INLAND borderland-and-minority edition. Held at 3 by the ≥50% co-financing requirement and the non-profit framing (needs the right applicant structure). Rolling submissions; board decides in July and December.
opportunities.territory: Translocal Europe — lead candidate: the Arbëreshë (Albanian-speaking, Byzantine-rite communities of Calabria/Basilicata/Sicily) in dialogue with Albania: endangered language, ritual laments, Easter Vallja dances
requiredDocs: Project description (translocal concept + 'bringing Europe together' angle)
Partners & letters of intent (cross-border + non-profit partner)
opportunities.notes: Rolling call — no hard deadline; deadline field points at the next (Dec 2026) board decision. Needs ≥50% co-financing and a non-profit + cross-border partner. Allianz Foundation site 403'd automated fetch; confirm terms at allianzfoundation.org.

opportunities.title: PhMuseum 2026 Women Photographers Grant
opportunities.budget: €10,000 total cash prize pool + publication, exhibition and portfolio visibility
opportunities.eligibility: Open worldwide to women and non-binary photographers 18+, professional or emerging. INLAND applies via a commissioned woman photographer presenting an edition's body of work.
opportunities.fitRationale: Documentary/visual-storytelling register squarely in INLAND's tradition, run by a respected Italian platform (Bologna). A prize, not production funding, and the applicant must be the photographer — works when a commissioned woman photographer carries an edition's series. Solid opportunistic 3.
opportunities.territory: Open — any in-progress INLAND edition's documentary body of work, carried by its woman photographer (e.g. women-led / ritual-lament threads from the Georgia, Tunisia or Rwanda/Angola research)
requiredDocs: Project series edit (cohesive body of work)
opportunities.notes: Reduced entry fee €25 until 19 Sept, €30 until 10 Oct; judging Oct–Nov, shortlist before 20 Nov. Confirm 2026 cash split and final entry date on the official grant page.

opportunities.title: Le Stanze della Fotografia — Open Call 2026 "Immagini e trame d'acqua" (water & heritage)
opportunities.budget: No cash grant confirmed — selection into a curated exhibition at Le Stanze della Fotografia, Venice (visibility / relationship foothold)
opportunities.eligibility: Free and open internationally to photographers, videomakers and writers (adults). INLAND (Italy-based, published) qualifies directly.
opportunities.fitRationale: Squarely in INLAND's register — place-based visual storytelling on water within cultural/landscape heritage, in INLAND's own photo+text+video format — but it is an exhibition selection, not edition funding. Best as a low-burden Venice visibility foothold and a way to field-test a water-heritage lead.
opportunities.territory: Italy — under-documented water cultures: Sardinia (Cabras/Santa Giusta reed-boat 'fassoni' fishers, bottarga, San Salvatore race) or the Po Delta (Comacchio eel-fishing 'casoni' & 'vallanti'); call also admits other UNESCO water heritage worldwide
requiredDocs: Selected images / video / text on the water-heritage theme
Short project statement tying work to a cultural/landscape (UNESCO-adjacent) water heritage
Author details (submit by email by 30 Sep 2026)
opportunities.notes: Free entry; submit by email (opencallstanze@gmail.com). Deadline 30 Sep 2026 unless extended. Official page 403'd automated fetch — re-verify specs, rights and any production/travel contribution on lestanzedellafotografia.it.

opportunities.title: Eidolon Grant 2026 — Vernacular / Everyday Photography (€25,000)
opportunities.budget: €25,000 total across two categories; spendable on fees, travel, preparation/production and partner costs
opportunities.eligibility: International, free entry — artists, scholars, researchers and professionals of vernacular photography. INLAND / a commissioned researcher-photographer eligible.
opportunities.fitRationale: Adjacent but real money on a free entry. Eidolon funds vernacular/archival image cultures rather than newly commissioned documentary, but a territory's own vernacular photographic memory (family albums, studio portraiture, migration photos) is exactly an archive of a place 'before it becomes history' — a well-framed proposal sits alongside a future edition.
opportunities.territory: Open — pair with a territory's vernacular photographic memory: highland Georgia/Caucasus (Soviet-era studio & family photography) or the Arbëreshë (diaspora family albums, Vallja/wedding/lament photographs)
requiredDocs: Project proposal (Contemporary or Historical category) + statement of past relevant work
Portfolio / prior work sample; author details
opportunities.notes: Free entry; deadline 30 Sep 2026 23:59. Confirm the exact split of the €25k across categories, whether an organisation (not only an individual) may apply, and whether newly commissioned (vs archive-based) work qualifies, on everydayphotography.org.

opportunities.title: Strategia Fotografia (MiC / DGCC) — national fund for commissioning & editorial photography [RADAR: 2026 closed, annual]
opportunities.funder: Ministero della Cultura — Direzione Generale Creatività Contemporanea (DGCC)
opportunities.budget: €2,000,000 total (2026 ref): €1.3M Acquisition & Commissioning, €700k Promotion (exhibitions + editorial photography productions)
opportunities.eligibility: Italian national public fund. Area 1 outputs must be assigned to an Italian public collection (needs a public-institution partner); confirm whether a private cultural producer/publisher may apply directly to Sections II–IV on the official bando.
opportunities.fitRationale: Arguably the single most on-profile Italian national fund for INLAND, currently absent from the board. Section IV (editorial photography productions) maps onto funding an INLAND issue; Section II (commissioning new work) onto an edition's field photography. Held at 4 by the Italian-public-collection condition and unconfirmed private-applicant eligibility.
opportunities.territory: Italy (or Italy-anchored) — the Arbëreshë (Calabria/Basilicata/Sicily), Sardinian pastoral & lagoon cultures, or the Appennine 'aree interne'; commission new work for a public collection paired with an editorial production (the INLAND issue)
requiredDocs: Project proposal per section (acquisition / commissioning / exhibition / editorial production)
Budget and work plan; partner Italian public collection (for Area 1)
Applicant credentials / track record; SPID/CIE registration on the DGCC Call Portal
opportunities.notes: RADAR / recurring annual fund — the 2026 (6th) edition CLOSED 15 Apr 2026. Deadline shown (~15 Apr 2027) is an ESTIMATE for the next edition; DGCC typically opens late Feb, closes mid-Apr. Not currently open — confirm 2027 dates, sections and eligibility on the official avviso before treating as actionable. Sister call to watch: Italian Council (closed 31 Mar 2026, also annual).

opportunities.title: National Geographic Society — Explorer Grants (Human Histories & Cultures / storytelling)
opportunities.budget: Level I up to US$20,000; Level II up to US$100,000 (field expenses, equipment, freelancer stipends)
opportunities.eligibility: Open worldwide to individuals 18+ with a record of media projects; a local collaborator is required when working outside your home community. INLAND applies via/with its commissioned edition photographer or writer, joining the NGS Explorer community.
opportunities.fitRationale: Squarely on-profile: the Human Histories & Cultures focus funds documenting living traditions, human geography and cultural continuity through photography, film and journalism — INLAND's exact register, at production scale. Applicant is the Explorer (INLAND's photographer/writer). Rolling with cyclical deadlines — confirm the current cycle.
opportunities.territory: Open — pairs with any in-progress/research INLAND edition. Lead candidate: Uzbekistan (research edition) — Boysun spring rituals (UNESCO ICH), Bukhara suzani embroidery & the last Bukharan-Jewish artisans, Karakalpak life on the dried Aral; each has named custodians, locatable places and a seasonal scene.
opportunities.notes: Rolling programme reviewed on cyclical deadlines (typically a few per year); the date shown is an indicative next checkpoint — confirm the current Level I/II cycle and any 'storytelling'/'documenting human traditions' special call on nationalgeographic.org/funding-opportunities. Note the local-collaborator requirement for work outside the applicant's home community.

opportunities.title: Pulitzer Center — Global Reporting Grants (underreported stories)
opportunities.budget: Typically US$5,000–10,000 for international travel (more or less by need)
opportunities.eligibility: Open worldwide to staff and freelance journalists; all media platforms (print, photography, audio, video). INLAND applies via its commissioned photographer/writer, with a letter of interest/commitment from a publishing outlet.
opportunities.fitRationale: Rolling, topic-open grant for in-depth, underreported stories in any location and any medium — a natural home for an INLAND field body of work if framed as an underreported cultural-continuity story with an outlet home. Journalism register and a required distribution/outlet commitment keep it at 3 rather than higher.
opportunities.territory: Open — pairs with a territory whose living-tradition story is genuinely under-reported. Lead candidate: Armenia (research edition) — Lake Sevan fishing cultures, duduk & lament (women's) oral tradition, and the cultural afterlife of displacement kept as background, not lens.
requiredDocs: Distribution plan + letter(s) of interest/commitment from outlet(s)
opportunities.notes: Rolling — no deadline; apply anytime. Date shown is a nominal placeholder, not a real cut-off. Requires a letter of interest/commitment from a publishing outlet and a distribution plan; register the INLAND edition photographer/writer as applicant. Confirm current terms on pulitzercenter.org/grant-application.
